metadata.purpose: Cross-disease epidemiological statistics for CVB-attributable disease burden
sources: Imazio 2015 (Circulation) — pericarditis incidence and colchicine trials
Richardson et al. 2009 — CVB pleurodynia/T1DM link

geo_searches.summary: GEO (Gene Expression Omnibus) searched April 2026 via Entrez API

enterovirus_AND_pancreatitis.note: No GEO datasets indexed with this exact term combination. Pancreatitis CVB studies appear primarily in clinical case series and autopsy literature rather than transcriptomic repositories.

enterovirus_AND_pericarditis.note: No GEO datasets for this combination. Pericarditis mechanistic data comes from NLRP3/IL-1 pathway studies (colchicine mechanism) rather than transcriptomics.

coxsackievirus_AND_hepatitis.note: No GEO datasets. CVB hepatitis (especially neonatal) is documented in case reports and autopsy series. Differs from HBV/HCV-focused transcriptomics literature.

datasets.title: Transcriptomic analysis of coxsackievirus B3 infection in induced pluripotent stem cell-derived brain-like endothelial cells
datasets.type: Expression profiling by high throughput sequencing
datasets.relevance: HIGH — CVB3 BBB penetration mechanism; directly relevant to meningitis pathogenesis

datasets.title: Polar infection of Echovirus 30 causes differential barrier affection and gene regulation at the blood-CSF barrier
datasets.type: Expression profiling by high throughput sequencing
datasets.relevance: HIGH — Blood-CSF barrier; Echovirus 30 is major aseptic meningitis pathogen

datasets.title: Diagnosing enterovirus meningitis via blood transcriptomics: an alternative for lumbar puncture?
datasets.type: Expression profiling by high throughput sequencing
datasets.relevance: VERY HIGH — Largest human transcriptomic dataset for enteroviral meningitis diagnosis; n=476

datasets.title: The differentially expressed genes induced by EV71 infection
datasets.relevance: MODERATE — EV71 (related enterovirus), CNS pathogenesis signatures

datasets.title: Enhanced RNAi does not provide efficient innate antiviral immunity in mice in vivo (smallRNA-Seq spleen)
datasets.type: Non-coding RNA profiling by high throughput sequencing
datasets.relevance: MODERATE — Innate immunity vs. CVB; spleen, not brain-specific

datasets.title: Enhanced RNAi does not provide efficient innate antiviral immunity in mice in vivo (organs small RNA-Seq)
datasets.type: Non-coding RNA profiling by high throughput sequencing

datasets.title: Enhanced RNAi does not provide efficient innate antiviral immunity in mice in vivo (ESCs TrapR small RNA-Seq)
datasets.type: Non-coding RNA profiling by high throughput sequencing

datasets.title: Enhanced RNAi does not provide efficient innate antiviral immunity in mice in vivo (ESCs small RNA-Seq)
datasets.type: Non-coding RNA profiling by high throughput sequencing

datasets.title: [MoGene-1_0-st] Affymetrix Mouse Gene 1.0 ST Array [transcript (gene) version]
[HG_U95Av2] Affymetrix Human Genome U95 Version 2 Array

pancreatitis.pathogen: Coxsackievirus B1, B4 (primary); B2, B3, B5 (secondary)
pancreatitis.mechanism: Direct acinar cell lysis via CVB replication; 2A/3C proteases activate trypsinogen cascade

cvb_attributable_fraction_pct.source: Hyoty 2016, Richardson 2009; CVB1/B4 isolated in 5-10% of acute cases

pancreatitis.sequelae: Exocrine insufficiency (15%), T1DM risk amplification (CVB-damaged islets adjacent to infected acinar tissue)
pancreatitis.t1dm_connection: CVB pancreatitis → bystander islet damage → T1DM risk elevation 2-4x
key_references: Hyoty H 2016 (Diabetologia) — CVB pancreatitis fraction
Parmar MS 2009 — viral acute pancreatitis review
Banks PA 2013 (Gut) — pancreatitis severity classification

pericarditis.mechanism: CVB pericardial infection → NLRP3 inflammasome → IL-1β → fibrinous exudate; autoimmune component via cardiac antigen exposure

cvb_fraction_of_viral_pct.source: Friman 1995, Kandolin 2015; CVB serology in pericarditis cohorts

recurrence_rates.source: COPE trial (Imazio 2005 Lancet) + CORP trial (Imazio 2011 Ann Int Med)

key_references: Imazio M 2015 (Circulation) — definitive incidence estimate
COPE trial 2005 — first colchicine RCT
CORP trial 2011 — colchicine recurrent pericarditis
Friman G 1995 — viral etiology in pericarditis

hepatitis.pathogen: Coxsackievirus B (all serotypes); most severe B1, B4
hepatitis.mechanism: Direct hepatocyte lysis; CVB uses CAR/DAF receptors expressed on hepatocytes; neonates lack maternal Ab protection

adult_disease.presentation: Elevated ALT/AST, RUQ pain, jaundice rare

neonatal_disease.source: Lake 1978, Modlin 1986; neonatal CVB sepsis/hepatitis series
neonatal_disease.mechanism: Vertical transmission perinatal → multi-organ CVB → DIC + hepatic necrosis + myocarditis
neonatal_disease.risk_factors: Maternal primary CVB infection near delivery → no transplacental IgG transfer

key_references: Modlin JF 1986 — neonatal enteroviral disease review
Lake AM 1978 — neonatal CVB hepatitis mortality
Abzug MJ 1995 — enteroviral hepatitis in neonates
Wreghitt TG 2002 — CVB hepatitis adult series

pleurodynia.pathogen: Coxsackievirus B (especially B3, B5); occasional CVB1, B2, B4
pleurodynia.mechanism: Direct CVB infection of intercostal and diaphragmatic muscles → acute pleuritis-like syndrome; NOT pericarditis or pleural effusion

t1dm_temporal_association.gamble_1980_finding: CVB pleurodynia outbreaks predict T1DM incidence spikes 3-10 years later
t1dm_temporal_association.gamble_paper: Gamble DR 1980 (Lancet) — 'Viral aetiology of type 1 diabetes'
t1dm_temporal_association.mechanism: Pleurodynia = sentinel symptom of CVB season; high CVB prevalence → more islet seeding → T1DM incidence rises years later
t1dm_temporal_association.richardson_2009: Richardson SJ 2009 — confirmed CVB in pancreata of T1DM cases using immunohistochemistry

key_references: Gamble DR 1980 (Lancet) — pleurodynia/T1DM association
Warin JF 1953 — UK pleurodynia epidemics
Artenstein MS 1964 — CVB pleurodynia outbreaks US
Richardson SJ 2009 — CVB in T1DM pancreas

aseptic_meningitis.pathogen: Enteroviruses (CVB1-B5, Echovirus 30, EV-D68, EV-A71); CVB fraction ~30-40%
aseptic_meningitis.mechanism: Enterovirus CNS invasion via DAF → choroid plexus transcytosis → CSF dissemination → meningeal inflammation

cvb_fraction_of_enteroviral_pct.source: Khetsuriani 2006 CDC surveillance; Echovirus 30 dominant in some years

aseptic_meningitis.sequelae_type: Cognitive impairment, hearing loss (infants most vulnerable)

neonatal_vs_adult.note: Age < 3 months: much higher risk of encephalitis, sepsis overlap

geo_datasets: GSE269413 — CVB3 iPSC-derived brain endothelium (BBB penetration; n=12)
GSE146890 — Echovirus 30 blood-CSF barrier (meningitis pathogenesis; n=15)
GSE133378 — Blood transcriptomics for EV meningitis diagnosis (n=476, largest dataset)
key_references: Rotbart HA 2000 — enteroviral meningitis review
Khetsuriani N 2006 (CDC) — US surveillance incidence
Jmor F 2008 — global aseptic meningitis epidemiology
Irani DN 1996 — CSF enterovirus PCR sensitivity

encephalitis.pathogen: CVB3, CVB4 (primary encephalitis); EV-A71, EV-D68 (brainstem encephalitis in children)
encephalitis.mechanism: CVB crosses BBB via infected monocytes (Trojan horse) and/or direct endothelial infection → parenchymal inflammation → neuronal necrosis

incidence_per_100k_year.source: Hosoya 2007, GBD 2019 — enteroviral fraction of all-cause encephalitis

encephalitis.brainstem_involvement: CVB3 rhombencephalitis described in immunocompromised patients
geo_datasets: GSE280993 — RNAi vs CVB in vivo, spleen smallRNA (n=8)
key_references: Hosoya M 2007 — enteroviral encephalitis in Japanese children
Sejvar JJ 2003 — encephalitis epidemiology
GBD 2019 — global encephalitis burden
Koskiniemi M 1997 — neurological sequelae enteroviral encephalitis

cross_disease_summary.interpretation: Absence from GEO reflects research gap, not absence of disease — pancreatitis/pericarditis CVB literature is clinical/serological rather than transcriptomic
